prompt.content: Triangle A can be moved onto Triangle B using only rigid motions. Which statement best explains why the triangles are congruent?

<svg viewBox='0 0 340 220' role='img' aria-label='Two congruent triangles where one is a translated and rotated image of the other'><line x1='20' y1='190' x2='320' y2='190' stroke='lightgray' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='20' y2='190' stroke='lightgray' stroke-width='1'/><polygon points='70,150 120,150 90,95' fill='none' stroke='black' stroke-width='3'/><text x='78' y='85' font-size='15'>Triangle A</text><polygon points='235,70 285,95 235,120' fill='none' stroke='black' stroke-width='3'/><text x='220' y='55' font-size='15'>Triangle B</text><path d='M 130 130 C 165 95, 185 85, 220 85' fill='none' stroke='gray' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5 5'/><text x='145' y='105' font-size='13' fill='gray'>rigid motions</text></svg>

choices.content: Rigid motions preserve side lengths and angle measures, so one triangle can match the other exactly.
Rigid motions make the image larger, so the triangles are congruent.
Congruent figures must always be in the same position on the plane.
The triangles are congruent because they have the same shape but different sizes.

steps.content: Rigid motions include translations, rotations, and reflections. These transformations preserve distance and angle measure, so if one figure can be mapped onto another using rigid motions, the figures are congruent.
